victim_side: Israeli civilians in the Oct. 7 southern-Israel attack
formula_note: Direct transfer from the NII Oct. 7 disaster subtotal.
uncertainty_note: Official subtotal, but it does not resolve all sub-site distributions.
quality_note: Strongest official civilian subtotal recovered for this bounded episode.

formula_note: Direct transfer from the recovered soldier-only fatality count.
uncertainty_note: Used because broader security tallies mix police and local-security personnel.
quality_note: Clean soldier-only numerator, but not an all-security-force total.

formula_note: 58 police + 10 Shin Bet = 68 explicitly identified non-soldier security deaths. Additional local-security officers are unresolved and not silently allocated.
uncertainty_note: Conservative minimum only; broader later security rolls suggest extra local-security fatalities, but this pass did not recover a matched denominator for them.
quality_note: Explicitly separated from the soldier-only DR.

formula_note: Lower bound = (63,978 Tekuma residents + 3,475 conservative Nova transient presence) * 4/30. Upper bound = 150,000 western-Negev southern-sphere residents * 4/30. Best = midpoint of the sourced lower and upper person counts, converted over 4 days.
uncertainty_note: The strongest remaining weakness is incomplete attacked-locality-by-locality resident reconstruction outside the Tekuma core and Nova site.
quality_note: Materially better than a whole-Israel denominator and transparent about the remaining geometry uncertainty.

formula_note: Low = 767 soldiers for all 4 days. Best = 767 on Oct. 7 plus an average of 1,500 soldiers in the attacked footprint on Oct. 8-10. High = 767 on Oct. 7 plus an average of 3,000 soldiers on Oct. 8-10. The reinforcement scenarios are anchored by the documented 767 floor, the documented rapid retake, and same-day evidence that one attacked city alone accumulated nearly 1,000 mixed security personnel.
uncertainty_note: High-sensitivity model. It deliberately excludes police, Shin Bet, and local-security teams, and it stays far below all-Israel or whole-Gaza-ring deployments.
quality_note: Publishable only as a soldier-only theater exposure scenario.

formula_note: DR = (soldier direct deaths / soldier person-months) / (civilian direct deaths / civilian person-months). Low uses the civilian low and soldier high denominators (minimizes DR); best uses the midpoint civilian scenario and 1,500-soldier reinforcement scenario; high uses the civilian high and soldier low denominators (maximizes DR). [corrected: formula was previously inverted (civ_rate/mil_rate); now uses standard mil_rate/civ_rate per definitions.md]
uncertainty_note: This is a publishable but low-quality soldier-only DR. It should not be read as an all-security-force DR because police, Shin Bet, and local-security personnel are kept explicit and outside the core denominator.
quality_note: Published because the numerator-denominator pair is now transparent and theater-bounded, but the military denominator remains scenario-sensitive.

victim_side: Israeli civilians in attacked towns, kibbutzim, roads, and the Nova/Reim festival area
formula_note: Partial-scope scenario extracted from the unmerged Episode B locality reconstruction. It covers the community/festival massacre phase and excludes a separate border-base Episode A split. Values therefore sit below or near the official 778-civilian combined-episode anchor.
uncertainty_note: This is not a replacement for the combined-episode civilian numerator. It is a within-episode scenario intended to approximate the towns/festival/community-massacre share while a clean Episode A counterpart is absent.
quality_note: Useful scenario only; not a canonical replacement for the combined episode.

victim_side: Israeli soldiers responding in town/festival/community fights
formula_note: Partial allocation scenario for the subset of soldier deaths tied to community/festival rescue and retake fighting rather than border-base defense. Best estimate follows the local reconstruction in the downloaded Episode B working note.
uncertainty_note: This is the weakest part of the scenario because many inquiry summaries do not cleanly separate base-defense fighting from community-response fighting.
quality_note: High-uncertainty scenario allocation, retained only as a labeled sub-episode heuristic.

victim_side: Police, Shin Bet, local-security, firefighters, and other ambiguous security categories in community fighting
formula_note: Scenario subtotal for non-IDF or classification-ambiguous security deaths in attacked towns and festival/rescue sites, based on the local Episode B reconstruction.
uncertainty_note: Unknown-status totals could shift materially if some mixed security site totals were actually IDF or if more local-security deaths were definitively identified.
quality_note: Scenario-only subtotal; not an audited full security-force classification.

victim_side: Civilians physically present in attacked towns, kibbutzim, roads, and festival areas
formula_note: Partial-scope civilian exposure scenario for the attacked locality set used in the Episode B working note. This is much narrower than the combined episode lower/upper footprint and is driven by town and festival attack-duration assumptions.
uncertainty_note: Exposure durations are modeled rather than observed, especially for Sderot, Ofakim, and smaller kibbutzim where some residents escaped quickly and others hid for many hours.
quality_note: Scenario denominator only; useful for sub-scope comparison, not for replacing the canonical combined episode denominator.

victim_side: Responding IDF soldiers and combat-deployed tactical police at town/festival rescue sites
formula_note: Responder-exposure scenario for community and festival sites only. It excludes the fixed border-garrison/base-defense denominator and therefore should not be compared directly to the canonical soldier-only denominator without noting the scope change.
uncertainty_note: Force peaks are better documented than average duration. Tactical police are included in this scenario exposure because the working note used a responder-denominator concept rather than a pure soldier roster.

formula_note: Scenario DR for the Episode B towns/festival/community-massacre subset only: (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months). Low = (20/240) / (775/1200) = 0.1290. Best = (35/160) / (745/1900) = 0.5579. High = (55/120) / (710/3000) = 1.9366.
uncertainty_note: This scenario is useful because it isolates the civilian-massacre/community-response slice, but it is not a canonical episode DR. Without a matching Episode A split, this must remain a subordinate scenario under the combined episode.
quality_note: Labeled sub-episode scenario only; do not compare as if it were the canonical whole-episode DR.
